prompt: Who was named NBA Finals MVP in 2023?
explanation: Nikola Jokic won Finals MVP as the Nuggets won their first title in 2023.

prompt: Which team did Kawhi Leonard lead to the 2019 championship after joining from San Antonio?
explanation: Kawhi Leonard led the Toronto Raptors to their first NBA title in 2019.

prompt: Which team swept the Cleveland Cavaliers in the 2018 NBA Finals?
explanation: The Golden State Warriors swept LeBron James's Cavaliers 4-0 in the 2018 Finals.

prompt: Who won back-to-back NBA MVP awards in 2019 and 2020 while playing for Milwaukee?
explanation: Giannis Antetokounmpo won consecutive MVP awards for the Milwaukee Bucks.

prompt: Which player scored a record 81 points in a single game for the Lakers in 2006?
explanation: Kobe Bryant's 81-point outburst against Toronto is the second-highest single-game total in NBA history.

prompt: Which coach won the most NBA championships, mostly with the Boston Celtics and Chicago Bulls/LA Lakers respectively?
options: Red Auerbach and Phil Jackson each have significant records
explanation: Phil Jackson holds the record for most championships won by a head coach, with 11; Red Auerbach won 9 with the Celtics.

prompt: Which coach has won the most NBA championships as a head coach?
explanation: Phil Jackson won 11 championships as head coach of the Bulls and Lakers.

prompt: Which team did Gregg Popovich coach to five NBA championships?
explanation: Gregg Popovich led the San Antonio Spurs to five titles between 1999 and 2014.

prompt: Which player recorded a 'Quadruple-Double' most recently in a notable statistical feat (points, rebounds, assists, blocks/steals all in double digits)?
explanation: Nikola Jokic recorded a rare quadruple-double during the 2023 playoffs run.

prompt: Which player won the 2022 NBA Finals MVP award with the Golden State Warriors?
explanation: Stephen Curry won his first Finals MVP as the Warriors beat the Celtics in 2022.

prompt: Which team traded for James Harden in 2012, sparking one of the league's most prolific scoring careers?
explanation: Harden was traded from Oklahoma City to Houston in 2012 and became a scoring machine there.

prompt: Which player won three consecutive NBA scoring titles from 2018 through 2020 with the Houston Rockets?
explanation: Harden led the league in scoring for three straight seasons with Houston.

prompt: Which team drafted Nikola Jokic in the second round of the 2014 draft, a legendary steal?
explanation: The Nuggets selected Jokic 41st overall in 2014, one of the greatest draft steals in NBA history.

prompt: Which player was named Defensive Player of the Year multiple times while playing for Golden State?
explanation: Draymond Green won the Defensive Player of the Year award in 2017.

prompt: Who was the Finals MVP when the Miami Heat won the 2013 championship?
explanation: LeBron James won Finals MVP as Miami beat San Antonio in a thrilling seven-game series.

prompt: Who hit a dramatic game-tying three-pointer in Game 6 of the 2013 NBA Finals for the Heat?
explanation: Ray Allen's clutch three-pointer forced overtime, and Miami went on to win the series.

prompt: Which team beat Miami in the 2014 NBA Finals, prompting LeBron's return to Cleveland?
explanation: The Spurs dominated the 2014 Finals, and LeBron returned to Cleveland that summer.

prompt: Which player was the Finals MVP for the San Antonio Spurs in their 2014 title run?
explanation: A young Kawhi Leonard won Finals MVP for his standout play against Miami in 2014.

prompt: Which player set the NBA's single-season three-point record before Stephen Curry broke it multiple times?
options: Ray Allen held the career record; Curry set season records
explanation: Ray Allen held the career three-point record until Curry surpassed it in 2021.

prompt: Which player surpassed Ray Allen as the NBA's all-time leader in made three-pointers?
explanation: Stephen Curry passed Ray Allen's career three-point record in December 2021.

prompt: Which team did the Golden State Warriors beat in the 2015 NBA Finals for their first title of the Curry era?
explanation: The Warriors beat LeBron's Cavaliers in six games to win the 2015 title.

prompt: Which team won a record 73 regular-season games in 2015-16, breaking the Bulls' record?
explanation: The Warriors went 73-9 in 2015-16, breaking the 1995-96 Bulls' record of 72 wins.

prompt: Which team had previously held the record for most regular-season wins before the 2015-16 Warriors, with 72?
explanation: The 1995-96 Chicago Bulls went 72-10, a record that stood for two decades.

prompt: Which player won the Sixth Man of the Year award multiple times playing for the Clippers and Sixth Man legend title?
explanation: Lou Williams won the Sixth Man of the Year award three times.

prompt: Which team acquired Anthony Davis in a trade with New Orleans in 2019, teaming him with LeBron James?
explanation: The Lakers traded for Anthony Davis in 2019 and won the 2020 championship together.

prompt: Which season did the Lakers, led by LeBron James and Anthony Davis, win the NBA championship in the 'bubble' due to COVID-19?
explanation: The Lakers won the 2019-20 title inside the NBA's COVID-19 'bubble' in Orlando.

prompt: Which player won Finals MVP for the Lakers' 2020 'bubble' championship?
explanation: LeBron James was named Finals MVP as the Lakers won the 2020 title in the Orlando bubble.

prompt: Which player recorded the first-ever 70-point game since Kobe Bryant's 81, doing so for the Wizards in 2024?
explanation: Damian Lillard scored 71 points for Milwaukee against Detroit in February 2024.

prompt: Which player scored 60 points in under 30 minutes off the bench for the Warriors against Indiana in 2016?
explanation: Klay Thompson scored 60 points in just 29 minutes, mostly off the bench, in a December 2016 game.

prompt: Which team acquired Kevin Durant, Kyrie Irving and James Harden together in the early 2020s, aiming for a superteam?
explanation: The Brooklyn Nets briefly had Durant, Irving and Harden together before trades broke up the trio.

prompt: Which team did Kevin Durant and Kyrie Irving join together in 2019 free agency?
explanation: Durant and Irving both signed with Brooklyn in the 2019 offseason.

prompt: Which player won the 2021 NBA Finals MVP for the Milwaukee Bucks?
explanation: Giannis was dominant in the 2021 Finals, scoring 50 points in the series-clinching Game 6.

prompt: How many points did Giannis Antetokounmpo score in the series-clinching Game 6 of the 2021 NBA Finals?
explanation: Giannis scored 50 points as the Bucks won their first title since 1971.

prompt: Which team traded away Luka Doncic in a surprising blockbuster deal in February 2025?
explanation: The Dallas Mavericks traded Luka Doncic to the Lakers in a shocking February 2025 deal.

prompt: Which player did the Mavericks receive in the trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Lakers?
explanation: Anthony Davis was the centerpiece the Mavericks received from the Lakers in the Doncic trade.

prompt: Which award did Victor Wembanyama win in his rookie season, 2023-24?
explanation: Victor Wembanyama won Rookie of the Year for the San Antonio Spurs in his debut season.

prompt: Which team won the 2024 NBA championship?
explanation: The Boston Celtics beat the Dallas Mavericks to win a record 18th championship in 2024.

prompt: Who was named Finals MVP for the Boston Celtics in 2024?
explanation: Jaylen Brown won Finals MVP as Boston clinched its 18th title, passing the Lakers' 17.

prompt: Which player has won MVP awards with the Denver Nuggets in 2021, 2022 and 2024?
explanation: Nikola Jokic has won three MVP awards for the Nuggets.

prompt: Which team's 'Bad Boys' identity defined their back-to-back championships in 1989 and 1990?
explanation: The Detroit Pistons, known for their physical style, won back-to-back titles in 1989 and 1990.

prompt: Which player led the Pistons' 'Bad Boys' as their star point guard?
explanation: Isiah Thomas was the leader of the Pistons' championship teams of 1989 and 1990.

statement: The 2015-16 Golden State Warriors set the record for most regular-season wins in NBA history.
explanation: They finished 73-9, breaking the 1995-96 Bulls' record of 72 wins.

statement: Nikola Jokic was drafted in the first round of the 2014 NBA draft.
explanation: Jokic was drafted 41st overall, in the second round, one of the biggest steals in draft history.

statement: Kawhi Leonard won NBA championships with both the San Antonio Spurs and Toronto Raptors.
explanation: Leonard won his first title with San Antonio in 2014 and led Toronto to the title in 2019.

statement: Ray Allen's clutch three-pointer in Game 6 of the 2013 Finals forced overtime for the Miami Heat.
explanation: Allen's shot tied the game late, and Miami went on to win in overtime and later the series.

statement: The Detroit Pistons' 'Bad Boys' teams won back-to-back championships in 1989 and 1990.
explanation: Detroit's physical style of play led to consecutive titles at the end of the 1980s.

statement: The Boston Celtics passed the Lakers for most all-time NBA championships in 2024.
explanation: Boston's 2024 title gave them 18, one more than the Lakers' 17.

statement: Luka Doncic was traded from Dallas to the Lakers in a deal centered around Anthony Davis.
explanation: The February 2025 trade sent Doncic to LA and Davis to Dallas.

statement: Victor Wembanyama was drafted by the Dallas Mavericks in 2023.
explanation: Wembanyama was drafted first overall by the San Antonio Spurs, not Dallas.

statement: Isiah Thomas led the Detroit Pistons to consecutive championships.
explanation: Thomas was the star point guard for Detroit's title runs in 1989 and 1990.

statement: Damian Lillard scored over 70 points in a single game for Milwaukee in 2024.
explanation: Lillard scored 71 points against Detroit in February 2024, joining an elite group of scorers.

fixture: NBA Finals Game 6, 2013 (Heat vs Spurs, regulation)
explanation: Ray Allen's clutch three sent the game to overtime tied 95-95; Miami won in OT.

prompt: Order these NBA championship-winning teams chronologically (earliest first).
events: Houston Rockets win consecutive titles with Olajuwon (1994)
San Antonio Spurs win their first title with Duncan (1999)
Miami Heat win their first title with LeBron (2012)
explanation: Order: Pistons 1989, Rockets 1994, Spurs 1999, Heat 2012.

prompt: Order these events in the Golden State Warriors dynasty chronologically (earliest first).
events: Warriors win their first title of the era (2015)
explanation: Order: draft Curry 2009, first title 2015, 73-win season 2016, Durant signing 2016 (after the record season).

prompt: Which player won back-to-back scoring titles for the Philadelphia 76ers around 2022-23?
explanation: Joel Embiid led the NBA in scoring in the 2021-22 and 2022-23 seasons.

prompt: Which player won NBA MVP in 2023, playing center for the Philadelphia 76ers?
explanation: Joel Embiid won his first MVP award in the 2022-23 season.

prompt: Which country is Joel Embiid originally from?
explanation: Joel Embiid was born in Cameroon before moving to the US to play basketball.

prompt: Which player is nicknamed 'The Beard' due to his distinctive facial hair?
explanation: James Harden's beard has become one of his signature traits.

prompt: Which team won the 2019 NBA championship over the Golden State Warriors?
explanation: The Toronto Raptors won their first title in franchise history in 2019.

prompt: Which injury to Kevin Durant during the 2019 Finals had major long-term consequences for his career?
explanation: Durant ruptured his Achilles during Game 5 of the 2019 Finals, sidelining him for over a year.

prompt: Which team signed Kevin Durant after his Achilles injury, trading for him in 2019?
explanation: Durant signed with the Brooklyn Nets in free agency in 2019 while still recovering from injury.

prompt: Which team did Kevin Durant get traded to in 2023 after his stint with Brooklyn?
explanation: Durant was traded to the Phoenix Suns midway through the 2022-23 season.

prompt: Which player won Rookie of the Year for the 2022-23 season?
explanation: Paolo Banchero of the Orlando Magic won Rookie of the Year for 2022-23.

prompt: Which player won Rookie of the Year for the 2023-24 season?
explanation: Victor Wembanyama's dominant rookie season with San Antonio earned him the award.

prompt: Which team won the 2022 NBA championship?
explanation: The Warriors beat the Celtics in six games to win their fourth title in eight years.

prompt: Which team did the Warriors beat in the 2022 NBA Finals?
explanation: Golden State defeated Boston in six games in the 2022 Finals.

prompt: Which player set the record for most three-pointers made in a single NBA Finals game?
explanation: Stephen Curry set the record with 7 three-pointers in a Finals game against Boston in 2022.

prompt: Which player was drafted first overall by Orlando in the 1992 draft after a legendary college career at LSU?
explanation: Shaquille O'Neal was drafted first overall by the Orlando Magic in 1992.

prompt: Which team did Shaquille O'Neal win his first championship with?
explanation: Shaq won his first title with the Lakers in 2000, the first of a three-peat.

prompt: Which team did Shaquille O'Neal win a championship with after leaving the Lakers?
explanation: Shaq won a fourth title with the Miami Heat in 2006, alongside Dwyane Wade.

prompt: Which player won Finals MVP for the Miami Heat's 2006 championship?
explanation: Dwyane Wade was dominant in the 2006 Finals, leading Miami to their first title.

prompt: Which player spent his entire career with the Miami Heat and is nicknamed 'Flash'?
explanation: Dwyane Wade, nicknamed 'Flash', played the vast majority of his career for Miami.

prompt: Which three players formed the Miami Heat 'Big Three' starting in 2010?
options: LeBron James, Dwyane Wade, Chris Bosh
LeBron James, Kobe Bryant, Dwyane Wade
Dwyane Wade, Shaquille O'Neal, Chris Bosh
LeBron James, Ray Allen, Dwyane Wade
explanation: LeBron James, Dwyane Wade and Chris Bosh formed Miami's 'Big Three' in 2010.

prompt: Which team won a championship immediately after the Miami Heat's 'Big Three' broke up in 2014?
explanation: The Spurs beat the Heat in the 2014 Finals, prompting LeBron's return to Cleveland.

prompt: Which player scored a franchise-record 62 points for the Portland Trail Blazers, later joining Milwaukee?
explanation: Damian Lillard set the Blazers' franchise scoring record before later being traded to Milwaukee.

prompt: Which team traded for Damian Lillard in 2023 after his long tenure in Portland?
explanation: Lillard was traded to the Milwaukee Bucks to team up with Giannis Antetokounmpo in 2023.

prompt: Which player hit a legendary buzzer-beating three-pointer to eliminate the Rockets in the 2019 playoffs?
explanation: Lillard's iconic series-winning shot over Houston became one of the most famous playoff moments.

prompt: Which team represented the Western Conference in the 2021 NBA Finals, losing to Milwaukee in six games?
explanation: The Phoenix Suns lost the 2021 Finals to the Milwaukee Bucks in six games.

prompt: Which player led the Phoenix Suns to the 2021 NBA Finals?
explanation: Chris Paul had a career-best playoff run leading Phoenix to the 2021 Finals.

prompt: Which player won the scoring title for the 2023-24 NBA season?
explanation: Luka Doncic led the league in scoring during the 2023-24 season.

prompt: Which team did Shai Gilgeous-Alexander lead to the top of the Western Conference in the mid-2020s?
explanation: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander became the franchise cornerstone for the rebuilding Oklahoma City Thunder.

prompt: Which award did Jaylen Brown win in the 2023-24 NBA Finals alongside the championship?
explanation: Jaylen Brown was named Finals MVP as the Celtics won the 2024 title.

prompt: Which two players form the core duo often called the 'Jays' for the Boston Celtics?
explanation: Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown have been the Celtics' star duo since the late 2010s.

prompt: Which player scored a franchise-record number of points in a playoff game for the Celtics during their 2024 title run?
explanation: Jayson Tatum has set multiple Celtics postseason scoring records during his career.

prompt: Which team did the Denver Nuggets beat in the 2023 Western Conference Finals?
explanation: Denver swept the Lakers in the 2023 Western Conference Finals en route to their title.

prompt: Which player was named Defensive Player of the Year for the 2023-24 season?
explanation: Rudy Gobert won his fourth Defensive Player of the Year award in 2023-24.

prompt: Which player has won the Defensive Player of the Year award a record four times?
explanation: Rudy Gobert tied the record for most Defensive Player of the Year awards, held previously by Mutombo, Wallace and Howard.

prompt: Which player has won the Most Improved Player award and later became an All-Star with Minnesota / Indiana teams?
explanation: Pascal Siakam won Most Improved Player in 2019 en route to a championship with Toronto.

prompt: Which player led the Indiana Pacers to the 2024 Eastern Conference Finals?
explanation: Tyrese Haliburton's play helped Indiana reach the Eastern Conference Finals in 2024.

prompt: Which team's arena is famously called 'The Garden' in New York?
explanation: Madison Square Garden, home of the Knicks, is often simply called 'The Garden'.
